title: Where to get the NGE dvds
display_title: Where to get the NGE dvds
wikitext:
[]: Where to get the NGE dvds?             Quote Post by Syn » Tue Mar 11, 2014 4:05 pm The Platinum collections seem to range from 100$ for used and 200+$ for un-used... Is there any legitimate site in which there is a reasonable price for unused copies? Thanks!

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Tue Mar 11, 2014 5:27 pm eBay, Amazon. I think the series is now OOP if I'm not mistaken?

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Tue Mar 11, 2014 5:55 pm You just have to keep looking...

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Mar 11, 2014 6:57 pm Syn, as someone who collects quite a bit of things, I'm going to break it to you, you're going to have to get comfortable with used goods. Neon Genesis Evangelion has been out of print for long enough a time that getting a brand new copy of the show for a reasonable price is very unlikely. Also, keep in mind that the price something LISTS for is not the price that something SELLS for. People may list Evangelion for $200 on eBay, but they typically sell for something like $70-$120. You just need to be patient and wait for a copy in good shape to be listed for auction with a low starting bid. You really, really need to warm up to sites like eBay in this instance. The majority of sales I've had through eBay have been very positive. Also, as a tip, if you go with something like eBay, try to get a lot that includes EoE, and be careful not to buy a bootleg. There are a lot of those floating around. They're easy to spot, they don't look like any of the legitimate releases. Just check svenge's site to make sure you get a real release: http://svenge.pcriot.com/

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Tue Mar 11, 2014 7:17 pm Note that the official UK R2 releases aren't listed in that site (and don't all even look the same as the US versions - e.g. the EoE/Death box has three DVDs rather than two with one of them being double-sided), but will widen your pool of possible purchases if you can handle R2 PAL DVDs. (Yes, I do realise that UK people are more likely to be able to handle R1 NTSC than US people handling R2 PAL - but for some folk it's a possibility.)

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Mar 11, 2014 7:24 pm I've heard bad things about the R2 Platinum release. I really wouldn't recommend going that road. Syn is in Canada, so they would be better off getting an R1 release.

[]:             Quote Post by ZapX » Tue Mar 11, 2014 9:06 pm I have Platinum thinpak in great condition if you want it. Looking to sell it for around $80. Also have D&R/EoE box set.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Mar 11, 2014 10:21 pm ZapX wrote: I have Platinum thinpak in great condition if you want it. Looking to sell it for around $80. Also have D&R/EoE box set.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Wed Mar 12, 2014 4:04 am The Eva Monkey wrote: I've heard bad things about the R2 Platinum release. I really wouldn't recommend going that road. Syn is in Canada, so they would be better off getting an R1 release.
